Section Title: **Press Releases** > Coal India’s supplies to Power Sector scale up 14% amid rising demand
Content:
The country’s total power generation was 9.5 percent higher, at an average of 4.53 Billion Units (BU), till 15th April’22 over same period last year. The increase was 400 million units per day against the average generation of 4.13 BU for the comparative period.
Coal fired power generation climbed to an average of 3.5 BU per day in April’22 fortnight, 9.4 percent more over last April’s average of 3.2 BU for same period. The uptick of 300 million units generation per day includes imported coal as well. The bulk though was fueled by CIL’s supplies.
Sequentially, the coal based generation growth of this period is higher by 5.4% compared to the average of 3.32 BU of the whole month of March’22

Content:
The country’s total power generation was 9.5 percent higher, at an average of 4.53 Billion Units (BU), till 15th April’22 over same period last year. The increase was 400 million units per day against the average generation of 4.13 BU for the comparative period.
Coal fired power generation climbed to an average of 3.5 BU per day in April’22 fortnight, 9.4 percent more over last April’s average of 3.2 BU for same period. The uptick of 300 million units generation per day includes imported coal as well. The bulk though was fueled by CIL’s supplies.

Section Title: **Press Releases** > Coal India’s supplies to Power Sector scale up 14% amid rising demand
Content:
The country’s total power generation was 9.5 percent higher, at an average of 4.53 Billion Units (BU), till 15th April’22 over same period last year. The increase was 400 million units per day against the average generation of 4.13 BU for the comparative period.
Coal fired power generation climbed to an average of 3.5 BU per day in April’22 fortnight, 9.4 percent more over last April’s average of 3.2 BU for same period. The uptick of 300 million units generation per day includes imported coal as well. The bulk though was fueled by CIL’s supplies.
Sequentially, the coal based generation growth of this period is higher by 5.4% compared to the average of 3.32 BU of the whole month of March’22

(CIL) reported a 16% year-on-year (YoY) decline in consolidated net profit to ₹7,166 crore for the third quarter (Q3 FY26) ending December 2025, largely due to a ₹2,201 crore one-time provision for wage revisions. Despite lower profits, the company declared a ₹5.50/share interim dividend. Revenue saw a slight dip of 3.76% YoY, but showed a 15.58% growth from the previous quarter.

Section Title: **Press Releases** > Coal India’s auction sales ride high in Apr-Nov’20: up 77%
Content:
E-auction sales for November’20 not only witnessed improved volume bookings at 9.4 MTs, clocking 23.7% growth last November but CIL could also net 30% premium over the notified prices.
This is a big leap from 13% premium the auctions fetched in October’20 when CIL for the first time in the present fiscal introduced add on over the notified price, after a six month hiatus, to gauge the market response.

Section Title: **Press Releases** > Coal India’s supplies to Power Sector scale up 14% amid rising demand
Content:
The country’s total power generation was 9.5 percent higher, at an average of 4.53 Billion Units (BU), till 15th April’22 over same period last year. The increase was 400 million units per day against the average generation of 4.13 BU for the comparative period.
Coal fired power generation climbed to an average of 3.5 BU per day in April’22 fortnight, 9.4 percent more over last April’s average of 3.2 BU for same period. The uptick of 300 million units generation per day includes imported coal as well. The bulk though was fueled by CIL’s supplies.
Sequentially, the coal based generation growth of this period is higher by 5.4% compared to the average of 3.32 BU of the whole month of March’22

executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the Coal India LNG substitution thesis for 2025-2026, summarizing how rising global LNG prices are driving increased demand for domestic coal in India's power sector and the resulting impact on Coal India.

projected_import_volume.description: Projected LNG import volume for India in 2026.

domestic_gas_price_ceiling.description: The government-mandated price ceiling for domestic natural gas for a specific period.

lng_import_data_source.description: The primary source for LNG import data, such as the Petroleum Planning & Analysis Cell (PPAC).

monthly_import_data_example.description: An example of monthly LNG import data, including volume and year-on-year change.

global_lng_supply_context.description: Information on the global factors affecting LNG supply and prices, such as disruptions in major producing countries like Qatar and Australia, and the impact of geopolitical events like the West Asia conflict on Asian energy strategies.

india_national_coal_production_analysis.description: Analysis of India's overall coal production, including the achievement of the 1 billion tonne production milestone for the second consecutive year in March 2026, and official production targets set by the Ministry of Coal.

government_policy_on_energy_security.description: Details on the Indian government's policies regarding energy security, including the strategic emphasis on increasing domestic coal production, efforts to control coal prices, and reforms in the coal sector.

power_sector_fuel_switching_dynamics.description: An examination of the coal versus gas switching trend in India's power sector. This includes an analysis of fuel-wise generation data from the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) for FY26, showing the increased reliance on thermal coal.

exchange_name.description: Name of the power exchange, e.g., Indian Energy Exchange (IEX).

total_electricity_volume_growth_yoy.description: Year-on-year growth in total electricity volume traded on the exchange for a specific period.

day_ahead_market_clearing_price.description: The market clearing price in the Day-Ahead Market (DAM) and its year-on-year change.

real_time_market_volume_growth_yoy.description: Year-on-year growth in the Real-Time Market (RTM) volume.

reporting_period.description: The financial reporting period, such as Q3 FY26.

net_profit.description: Consolidated net profit for the period, including year-on-year change.

ebitda.description: Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization for the period, including year-on-year change.

key_financial_factors.description: Significant factors impacting financial results, such as one-time provisions.

dividend_declared.description: Details of any interim or final dividend declared for the period.

period.description: The period for which the e-auction data is being reported (e.g., FY26, March 2026).

average_premium_over_notified_price.description: The average premium percentage achieved in e-auctions over the base notified price.

allocation_rate.description: The percentage of offered coal quantity that was successfully allocated in the auction.

average_realized_price_per_ton.description: The average selling price per ton of coal realized in the e-auction.

coal_india_production_and_offtake_figure.description: Specific operational data for Coal India, including monthly and annual production volumes for FY26, offtake performance, and supplies directed to the power sector.

comparative_cost_analysis_coal_vs_lng.description: A direct comparison of the cost-effectiveness of using domestic coal versus imported LNG for power generation in India during the 2025-2026 period, based on prevailing market prices.

investment_thesis_outlook_and_validation.description: A concluding synthesis that validates the LNG substitution thesis based on the evidence presented. It provides a forward-looking outlook for the remainder of 2026 regarding coal demand and Coal India's performance.

key_data_points.description: A list of crucial quantitative data points found in the research, such as specific LNG prices, coal production tonnages, e-auction premiums, power exchange volumes, and financial figures for quick reference.

body_name.description: The name of the government or regulatory body (e.g., Ministry of Coal, PPAC).

role_description.description: A brief description of the body's role in the energy sector.

key_report_or_data.description: The name of a key report or dataset published by the body (e.g., Ready Reckoner, Monthly Generation Report).

key_regulatory_bodies_and_reports.description: A list of the key government and regulatory bodies involved, such as the Ministry of Coal, Petroleum Planning & Analysis Cell (PPAC), Central Electricity Authority (CEA), and CERC, along with the names of their relevant reports (e.g., Ready Reckoner).

potential_risks_to_thesis.description: An analysis of potential risks and countervailing factors that could weaken the LNG substitution thesis, such as a sharp drop in global LNG prices, significant increases in renewable energy generation, or changes in government policy.
